I came here riding my motorbike as fast as I could,” said José Morillo, as he arrived outside a block of flats called Residencias Obelisco . Continue reading...

events.title: Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three
events.description: Investigators says one of two teenagers who allegedly opened fire on students at high school was regular player Philippine authorities have temporarily blocked the online gaming app GoreBox days after a rare school shooting in the south-east Asian country killed three students and injured 20 others. Investigators said that one of the two teenagers accused of opening fire on students at San Jose National high school in Tacloban city had regularly played the game, which allows players to use various weapons and depicts graphic violence. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three

events.title: Venezuela earthquake: powerful back-to-back quakes collapse buildings in capital Caracas
events.description: Homes and buildings collapse after double quake, with tremors felt in neighbouring Colombia Venezuela earthquakes: follow live updates Venezuela’s capital, Caracas, has been struck by two powerful earthquakes that caused major damage at the international airport and brought several large buildings crashing down. The quakes – among the largest in Venezuela’s history – occurred in quick succession and were felt in many parts of the country. But the worst destruction appeared to have taken place in and around Caracas where videos on social media showed scenes of panic as passengers raced through the corridors of Maiquetía airport seeking cover from falling debris. Continue reading...

events.title: Global learning losses threaten an entire generation
events.description: Conflict, displacement and climate shocks are disrupting education for an estimated 258 million school-aged children and adolescents worldwide, raising fears that millions risk losing not only years of schooling but future opportunities altogether, according to a new report released Tuesday.

events.title: Power outages and melting roads: Heatwave strains French infrastructure

Electricity provider EDF says it has had to reduce its nuclear power output, but that it still has enough capacity to meet demand. Also in the show - the UN's International Maritime Organization starts evacuating some of the 11,000 sailors stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, and Europe's defence industry prepares for a boost with the IPO of Franco-German tankmaker KNDS.

events.title: UN chief: Targeting methane is a climate battle ‘we can win’

events.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor who had worked in DRC
events.description: French health ministry says patient’s contacts are being traced and that risk to European public is very low The first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement . “All precautionary measures, including the patient’s isolation, were taken upon his arrival in the country, with transfer to the hospital under secure conditions to prevent any risk of contamination.” Continue reading...

events.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
events.description: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

events.title: World Cup 2026: Vinicius Junior scores twice as Brazil beat Scotland to reach last 32
events.description: Vinicius Junior scored twice as Brazil eased to a 3-0 win over Scotland on Wednesday, sealing top spot in Group C and a place in the World Cup last 32. Matheus Cunha added a third, while Neymar made his first international appearance in more than two-and-a-half years after returning from injury.

events.title: Lives at risk in DR Congo as Ebola outbreak continues to outpace response

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lives at risk in DR Congo as Ebola outbreak continues to outpace response

events.title: Play puts spotlight on Kenya’s crisis of gender-based violence
events.description: Autobiographical work Free Me aims to encourage victims to speak out in country where violence against women is rising There are audible gasps in the auditorium in Nairobi as a husband launches a volley of blows and slaps on his wife and pushes her to the floor. “I wish I could spare you this,” the wife tells the audience. “My husband beat me up as if we were in a bar fight. Except, in a bar someone fights back.” The scene comes from Free Me, an autobiographical play by Gathoni Kimuyu, a Kenyan theatre and TV producer who lived through an abusive marriage. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia politics live: PM warns against drift to ‘edges of the mainstream’ in allusion to Karl Stefanovic podcasts
events.description: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Mike Burgess says he’s really concerned about the level some nation-states, like Iran, could go to cause harm against Australians. Speaking to ABC RN Breakfast, the spy boss says the security level is worsening and we could see an Australian killed at the hands of a foreign government. Targeted killing … that could be a prominent Australian that is killed or everyday Australians just about going about their business. So I am really concerned about the actions of some nation-states, Iran in particular, and the level they will go to. That’s why we describe our security environment as degraded because some nation-states will plumb the depths and go to extreme levels that we would find unacceptable and horrible. At this point in time with a dynamic, diverse integrated security environment, we are really concerned about this. To talk about multicultural Australia is to just talk about modern Australia, to talk about who we are and who we’ve always been. And I find it really odd. I saw Angus Taylor unable to answer the question. Like it’s a really simple question. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia politics live: PM warns against drift to ‘edges of the mainstream’ in allusion to Karl Stefanovic podcasts

events.title: Brazilian federal police arrest Spanish citizen at São Paulo airport for racism

Brazil has some of the strictest anti-racism laws in Latin America. Insulting a person on the basis of race carries a penalty of imprisonment from two to five years and a fine. Continue reading...

events.title: World Cup 2026: Morocco complete comeback against Haiti in Group C clash
events.description: Morocco beat Haiti 4-2 in a dramatic Group C encounter in Atlanta, in a match that was level at half-time after a breathless first period. Achraf Hakimi and Ismaël Saibari restored parity before Soufiane Rahimi and Gessime Yassine struck late to seal victory, as Haiti were ultimately undone by Morocco’s late pressure and attacking depth.

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on children and armed conflict
events.description: The UN Security Council held on open debate on Thursday focused on strengthening protections for children caught in armed conflict. The Secretary-General's latest report reveals that in 2025, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups, marking a first in 30 years of UN monitoring. The report verified 38,558 grave violations such as killing, recruitment and abduction, affecting 24,174 children, many of whom suffered multiple violations.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Security Council LIVE: Open debate on children and armed conflict

events.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
events.description: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with 23mm reported in Kranj. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia

events.title: Last Islamic State-linked Australian woman from Syrian camp to return despite previous ban

The home affairs minister, Tony Burke, said the woman would face an unprecedented level of security monitoring once she arrives in the country, including constant monitoring and requirements to report to authorities regularly. Kerim Alajbegovic opened the scoring with a stunning strike, before an own goal from Mahmoud Abunada doubled their advantage. events.title: UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told
events.description: Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to ‘pressure’ from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan’s civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. In May 2024, officials from the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) told Nathaniel Raymond , an American human rights investigator at Yale University, that “significant private pressure” from the UAE meant the UK would not publicly divulge information linking Ethiopia and the emirates to their support for the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told

events.title: France experiences its hottest day since measurements began in 1947

Around 44 million of the nearly 67 million people in France are currently under the highest red alert level for heat. FRANCE 24's Luke Shrago reports.

events.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
events.description: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility , with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a widespread Ebola outbreak . Continue reading...

events.title: Duck off: World Cup hero Merlín barred from Mexico game due to Fifa’s animal ban
events.description: World’s sixth-most famous duck falls short of dream Two-year-old must watch Czechia game from distance Merlín the duck ’s bid to see his beloved Mexico live has hit a snag after he was barred from El Tri’s match with Czechia on Wednesday. Merlín has become a folk hero in Mexico after becoming a symbol of El Tri’s World Cup campaign on home soil. His fans had launched a campaign for Merlín to attend Wednesday’s match alongside his human family. But his journey was cut short by Fifa regulations. Continue reading...

events.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive
events.description: Written in 1947, Kiyoshi Tanimoto’s account of the horrors of the atomic bomb attack will be published in August and is being made into a film The memoir of a man who survived the horrors of Hiroshima is to be published for the first time this summer after its discovery in a US archive. The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a feature film by Takehiro Hira, whose acclaimed roles include the detective in the Netflix Japanese-British drama Giri/Haji. Pre-production begins in November, ahead of the shoot in February 2027. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive

events.title: Greetings from sweltering Switzerland
events.description: On the waterfront in Lucerne, Switzerland, soccer fans watched jumbo TVs showing a World Cup match played an ocean away. But the air felt more like the tropics.

events.title: As U.S. government looks to leverage food aid, producers seek other delivery methods
events.description: The U.S. is changing the way food aid is distributed to starving children around the world. It is now using aid as a diplomatic tool as nutrition bars pile up in U.S. factories.

events.title: How Big Tech is changing journalism
events.description: Technology companies are shaping how news is presented and digested, leaving media outlets little option but to comply. At DW's Global Media Forum in Bonn, participants discussed how to resist and adapt.

events.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
events.description: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Switzerland edge past Canada in Group B decider
events.description: Switzerland claimed a narrow 2-1 victory over Canada at BC Place in Vancouver to secure top spot in Group B. Ruben Vargas and Johan Manzambi scored for the Swiss, while Promise David's late effort proved only a consolation as Canada fell short in a tightly contested encounter between two evenly matched sides.

events.title: Can Trump's Iran deal survive?
events.description: 👉Listen to The World with Richard Engel and Yalda Hakim on your podcast app👈

events.title: 2 missing Chicago men found dead outside Mexico City, family confirms
events.description: A missing Chicago couple were among a number of bodies found last week on the outskirts of Mexico City, their family confirmed on Wednesday

events.title: Young peacebuilders: Funding matters, but so do trust and partnerships
events.description: Young people working on promoting peace in some of the world’s most fragile and polarized places say financial support remains essential, but money alone will not sustain their efforts to organize youth to participate in peacebuilding.

events.title: Illegal immigrant who entered US on visa to visit Disney World arrested after overstaying a decade, sex crime
events.description: An Argentine citizen who overstayed his U.S. visa by a decade after visiting Disney World was arrested in Maryland, DHS says. He has assault conviction.

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: Meet the man Israel chose to be its first-ever ambassador to the Christian world
events.description: Ambassador George Deek explains the mission behind his new role as special envoy to the Christian world and Israel's efforts to strengthen the Judeo-Christian bond.

events.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
events.description: Experts say Trump is likely to ignore it, but that even though it carries little power, it is politically significant.

events.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
events.description: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

events.description: NATO chief Mark Rutte points to use of US bases in Europe as he stresses support for US war on Iran.

events.title: Trump White House requests $87.6bn in spending, including for Iran war
events.description: The request is likely to face an uphill battle in Congress, which voted a day earlier to rein in Trump's war powers.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
events.description: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been given no choice. They thought it would be a temporary evacuation after Indonesia had won independence. Continue reading...

events.title: Inside the gold-mining town where the Ebola outbreak likely started
events.description: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

events.title: What does British PM's resignation mean for European security?
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What does British PM's resignation mean for European security?

events.title: India news: Protests in northern region Ladakh over talks with Delhi
events.description: Businesses in both northern Leh and Kargil districts remained shut to protest the alleged retraction of key constitutional and administrative agreements. Follow DW for more.

events.title: China detains two Japanese nationals on smuggling charges
events.description: China has detained two Japanese nationals on suspicion of smuggling banned goods. Reports suggest that at least one case may involve strategic rare-earth-related exports.

events.title: Haiti - Morocco: five-goal thriller with late Atlas Lions surge
events.description: Morocco faced Haiti in a decisive final Group C match and secured a 4-2 victory in a chaotic, end-to-end encounter. However, with Brazil beating Scotland 3-0 and holding a superior goal difference, the Seleção finish top of the group, leaving Morocco to qualify in second place. In a thrilling match that saw both sides trade goals, the Atlas Lions eventually pulled away thanks to their late attacking depth. Relive the match to see how it unfolded.

events.title: NATO needs to see a credible trajectory for when the UK will be ready for war - and that is no longer in Sir Keir's gift
events.description: Sir Keir Starmer's determination to release a long-delayed defence investment plan ahead of a NATO summit next month risks doing more harm than good as officials scramble to meet what has become a political deadline.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 NATO needs to see a credible trajectory for when the UK will be ready for war - and that is no longer in Sir Keir's gift

events.title: Trains across Germany briefly halted after communication system issue
events.description: Germany's railway system was brought to a halt on Tuesday evening, leaving passengers stranded across the country following an IT issue.

events.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman, 5 children after decade of alleged captivity
events.description: Police in Pakistan have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for over a decade

events.title: New Security Council resolution upholds accountability for attacks against peacekeepers
events.description: The United Nations Security Council unanimously adopted a resolution on Tuesday that calls for accountability for crimes committed against UN peacekeepers.

events.title: Air Canada flight with 61 passengers diverts to Boston after captain is removed from cockpit midair
events.description: An Air Canada flight diverted to Boston's Logan Airport after the captain suffered a midair medical emergency, according to airline officials.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Air Canada flight with 61 passengers diverts to Boston after captain is removed from cockpit midair

events.title: Seafood worker charged in stabbing near Nantucket ferry docks says video backs self-defense in wealthy enclave
events.description: Seafood worker accused of stabbing near Nantucket ferry docks claims police video shows self-defense after being attacked from behind, attorney says.

events.title: Woman linked to transgender Zizian cult charged with arranging her parents' murders on her 30th birthday
events.description: A woman linked to the cultlike Zizians group is charged with murder and conspiracy in the deaths of her parents. Prosecutors say she had help.

events.title: ‘Free Karmelo’ mob hunted woman in bloody attack as thugs shouted support for killer, victim says
events.description: A Texas woman said a group of women jumped her in Longview while yelling 'Free Karmelo,' saying they targeted the smallest white girl they could find.

events.title: Barry Morphew used fake name, lived with woman until arrest in wife’s murder case exposed him: police
events.description: Barry Morphew allegedly used the fake name "Lee Moore" while living in Arizona before his arrest on first-degree murder charges in his wife's death.

events.title: France reports first Congo outbreak linked Ebola case
events.description: France has confirmed its first Ebola case linked to the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The infection comes weeks after European health authorities said there were no active Ebola cases in the EU.

events.title: France reports first Ebola case
events.description: France has reported its first case of Ebola during the latest outbreak after a doctor returning from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) tested positive.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Four in five under-16s in Australia using social media despite ban, study shows
events.description: Experts say law not enough to stop children accessing harmful content online and more ‘convincing strategy is required’ More than 80% of under-16s in Australia said they were still using social media three months after legislation banning them from it came into force, research shows. Australia is the first country to ban social media for children. Since December 2025, under-16s have been prohibited from having accounts with many social media platforms including TikTok, X, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube and Snapchat. Continue reading...

events.title: U.S. lifts Iran oil sanctions. And, federal judge rules SAVE voter tool unlawful
events.description: The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.

events.title: Who would pay for Trump's proposed $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund?
events.description: It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."

events.title: Is xenophobia in South Africa risking investment?
events.description: Vigilante groups and right-wing populist forces are stoking anti-immigrant sentiment and demanding that they leave the country by June 30. Experts fear serious economic implications for the country and the region.

events.title: France, UK and Spain see record temperatures as heatwave grips western Europe
events.description: Tens of millions of people are grappling with punishing temperatures, which have led to red heat alerts across the continent.

events.title: Eight sentenced to 450 years in prison over anti-ICE riot where officer was shot
events.description: An officer was shot in the neck during the Texas disorder by "Antifa Cell operatives", prosecutors said.

events.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
events.description: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire

events.title: Leader of secretive South Korean church arrested in election influence investigation
events.description: The 95-year-old leader of a secretive South Korean church has been arrested as authorities widened an investigation into allegations that he illegally recruited thousands of followers into the conservative People Power Party to influence elections

events.title: Ebola in DR Congo: first month of outbreak sees record number of cases – UN humanitarians
events.description: Ebola has been spreading at unprecedented speed in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), bringing risk and fear into people’s daily lives, UN humanitarians said on Tuesday.

events.title: Woman upset over Taco Bell wait time allegedly flashed gun, challenged workers to fight outside
events.description: A Maryland woman allegedly revealed a handgun and threatened Taco Bell employees during a dispute over wait time, leading to her arrest on assault charges.

events.title: Minor crash, stolen Buffalo Wild Wings food lead deputies to alleged murder plot against teen's parents
events.description: A struck road sign and stolen Buffalo Wild Wings food in Virginia allegedly led deputies to uncover a detailed murder plot against a teen suspect's parents.

events.title: Judge sentences New York high school senior who set homeless man on fire on subway
events.description: Hiram Carrero, 19, set the sleeping homeless man on fire in December 2025. Carrero's victim was left with severe burns and permanent damage to his body.

events.title: US military conducts strike on another vessel carrying alleged narco-traffickers, killing 2
events.description: The U.S. military announced a lethal strike on a vessel in the Caribbean allegedly carrying narco-traffickers, killing two people and leaving six survivors.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military conducts strike on another vessel carrying alleged narco-traffickers, killing 2

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Victorian Liberal party in turmoil as MP denies colleague’s accusations of assault
events.description: Police confirm they are investigating as sources close to accused MP say he vehemently denies the allegations and is considering legal action Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A female Victorian Liberal MP has accused a member of her party room of assaulting her at a community event, throwing the party into turmoil just five months out from the state election. Sources close to the accused MP said he vehemently denies the allegation and is considering a defamation legal action against his colleague. Continue reading...

events.title: Rubio assures Gulf allies US will protect interests in Iran peace talks
events.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Wednesday assured Gulf allies that Washington would protect their interests as it pursues a final settlement with Iran. President Donald Trump said talks were progressing well, while requesting nearly $88 billion in supplemental funding for the Pentagon after Congress passed a resolution urging an end to hostilities.

events.title: Europe leaders reaffirm unity on defence ahead of Ankara NATO summit
events.description: European leaders met Wednesday in Berlin ahead of next month's NATO summit in Ankarai to discuss European defence and security guarantees for Ukraine, following tensions with the United States and among the main European members.

events.title: Knicks fan’s trash can grab ends with fines, firing and walk of shame return trip for JPMorgan Chase exec
events.description: New York City sanitation police issued two summonses to the former JPMorgan Chase executive who took a Knicks trash can from a parade route.

events.title: California sues Trump EPA over move to subject state vehicle emissions waivers to congressional review
events.description: California sues the Trump administration after the EPA moved to subject the state's vehicle emissions waivers to congressional review under the CRA.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Japan says China has detained 2 of its citizens suspected of smuggling banned items
events.description: China has detained two Japanese citizens suspected of smuggling prohibited items, reportedly linked to rare earths

events.title: How the stories we tell about ageing shape our future
events.description: The way societies talk about ageing can shape everything from public policy to people's own expectations of later life, the United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE) said on Wednesday in a new report that calls for shifting away from narratives that portray older persons as a burden.

events.title: Powering resilience: How Indonesia is building a more secure energy future
events.description: As countries grapple with energy price shocks, supply disruptions and the growing impacts of climate change, energy security has become a pressing concern.

events.title: Whistleblower lawsuit says neurosurgeons left patients anesthetized and safety concerns ignored
events.description: Former surgical leaders allege in a whistleblower lawsuit that a neurosurgeon fell asleep during surgery at an Illinois hospital in Rockford amid other disturbing claims.

events.title: Trump says UK's Starmer will resign, wishes him well amid reports of PM's political future
events.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer will step down, wishing the foreign leader well, while also leveling criticism.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Dior moves Paris men’s show earlier as heat wave grips city
events.description: Dior has moved its men’s Paris Fashion Week show to 9 a.m. to avoid extreme heat

events.title: Keir Starmer resigns as British prime minister after devastating Labour revolt and local election losses
events.description: British PM Keir Starmer announced his resignation after Labour's devastating local election losses and a mounting revolt from within his own party ranks.

events.description: It is suing the US defence department after it was added to a list of firms with ties to the Chinese military.

events.description: The BBC spoke with Stanford University graduates about what they really think about artificial intelligence.

events.title: Olympic athletes to get cash grants from new $100m fund created by IOC
events.description: Olympians from both the summer and winter games can apply for $10,000 grants after competing.

events.description: Madibo's tackle on Kone in the team's Group B match left the Canadian player with a broken leg.

events.title: Katz: Israel will not withdraw from Lebanon even if US demands it
events.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz said Israel will maintain its presence in Lebanon even if US demands withdrawal.

events.description: Iranian team's trip to Seattle was delayed by 25 minutes as Taremi and Alhoei were held up for an undisclosed reason.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news

events.title: Colombia’s leftwing candidate concedes election to Trump-endorsed millionaire
events.description: Leftist Iván Cepeda conceded to far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella who won by razor-thin margin The defeated leftwing candidate in Colombia’s presidential runoff has conceded to the far-right, Trump-admiring millionaire lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella. Since Sunday night, the preliminary count had already pointed to a De la Espriella victory by a razor-thin margin of less than 1% of the vote. Continue reading...

events.title: Dettol apologises after ‘toxic men’ advert sparks backlash in China
events.description: British disinfectant brand withdraws advert about a man’s efforts to find a ‘clean and untouched’ woman The British hygiene brand Dettol has apologised after an advertisement released in China, which it said was intended to criticise “toxic men”, was widely condemned on social media as offensive to women. The five-minute advert for a multipurpose disinfectant, released across many online platforms at the end of May, features a man comparing his girlfriend with his former partner. Learning that his former girlfriend previously lived with someone else, the man likens their relationship to a “secondhand service”. He then tells his friends that he intends to find a “clean and untouched” woman for whom he can be the first sexual partner. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dettol apologises after ‘toxic men’ advert sparks backlash in China

events.title: Archaeologists find huge Viking textile production site in Denmark
events.description: Archaeologists have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society.

events.title: Afghan Taliban hold first, closed-door talks with EU on deportations
events.description: Rights groups criticized the meeting, saying it undermines the EU's human rights obligations.

events.title: Germany's Merz rallies European NATO allies ahead of summit
events.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz hosted leaders of European NATO powers in Berlin to hash out consensus on bolstering European defense. With US tensions looming, the leaders signaled that transatlantic ties are improving.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany's Merz rallies European NATO allies ahead of summit

events.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
events.description: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.
events.ticker_label: 💥 European heat wave is not normal summer weather

events.title: Climate change: Heatwave costs the German economy billions
events.description: Never before has a June been as hot in Germany as in 2026. This has consequences not only for people and nature, but also for businesses. Heat reduces productivity and causes illness, while cooling is expensive.

events.title: Southeast Asia boost defense ties but avoids firm alliances
events.description: Countries across the region are expanding defense cooperation in ways that change the South China Sea equation but fall short of a new anti-China bloc.

events.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
events.description: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

events.title: North Korean soldier crosses border to South
events.description: South Korea's military says it is investigating the soldier's reasons for crossing the inner-Korean border. While dozens of North Koreans flee each year, usually via China, crossing directly south is very rare.

events.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
events.description: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

events.title: How Africa's youth wants to save democracy
events.description: Africa's young population often finds itself facing aging, autocratic rulers. Unwilling to settle for democratic rituals alone, many are searching for creative new ways to shape their future.

events.title: Modigliani nude sets European auction record at London art sale
events.description: A London auction of artworks from billionaire Joe Lewis's private collection raised $392.6 million on Wednesday, led by an Amedeo Modigliani nude portrait that set a European auction record. The first sale featured 48 works by artists including Pablo Picasso, René Magritte and Gustav Klimt. Lewis, former majority owner of Tottenham Hotspur, transferred his stake to a family trust in 2022.

events.title: Did Qatar's prime minister 'snub' JD Vance? Doha, Washington deny claim
events.description: A viral clip from the first round of US-Iran peace talks in Switzerland fuelled online speculation that US Vice President JD Vance was snubbed by Qatar’s Prime Minister, after users claimed the video shows him "ignore" Vance in favour of greeting Pakistan’s Prime Minister. The Qatari PM though, dismissed the claims as “unfounded,” saying he and Vance had already spent hours together during the talks. The White House also rejected the narrative, pointing the finger at Iran for the rumours.

events.title: Trump pressures congressional Republicans by delaying landmark housing bill over voter ID push
events.description: US President Donald Trump announced Wednesday that he would not sign a new landmark housing bill until congressional Repulicans pass his proposed SAVE America Act to restrict voting procedures which he has been calling for for months.

events.title: Ukraine destroys key rail bridge as it aims to 'isolate Crimea'
events.description: Ukraine said its forces destroyed a key railway bridge in Crimea, as it seeks to isolate the Russian-occupied peninsula.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Ukraine destroys key rail bridge as it aims to 'isolate Crimea'

events.title: 'Worrying' study shows Australia's social media ban has made no 'meaningful difference' to teenagers
events.description: Some 85% of under-16s are still using social media in Australia despite a ban being put in place in December, a major new study suggests.

events.title: Zimbabwe's Senate approves bill to delay the presidential election and overhaul the vote
events.description: Zimbabwe’s Senate has approved constitutional amendments that would remove direct presidential elections and extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term

events.title: Hong Kong police arrest booksellers on suspicion of selling seditious publications
events.description: Hong Kong police have arrested two people on suspicion of selling seditious publications and receiving funds from foreign political organizations

events.title: Progressive candidate concedes Colombian presidential election to Trump-backed outsider
events.description: Iván Cepeda has conceded Colombia’s presidential election to Abelardo de la Espriella, a conservative outsider endorsed by U.S. President Donald Trump

events.title: Andy Burnham inches closer to power in Britain as Keir Starmer seeks a legacy
events.description: Andy Burnham is closer to becoming Britain’s next prime minister without a contest

events.title: German railway says outage that halted all trains was caused by scheduled work
events.description: Germany's railway network operator says a late-night communication system outage left trains and travelers stranded around Germany was caused by workers swapping out a component

events.title: Archaeologists find huge Viking textile production site in Denmark
events.description: Archaeologists say they have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society

events.title: South African prisons open art galleries to showcase inmates' work
events.description: In Johannesburg, a correctional facility is transforming perceptions with a gallery showcasing inmates' artwork

events.title: Pennsylvania mother's video captures armed intruder holding her toddler as she begs for child's release
events.description: A jury convicted Antonio Hammond of kidnapping a 2-year-old girl during an hours-long standoff in Harrisburg after he held the child at gunpoint.

events.title: Three Wendy's employees allegedly took food from trash, spat on it before serving unhappy customer
events.description: Three Wendy's employees in South Carolina were arrested for allegedly pulling food from trash and spitting on it before serving it to a customer.

events.title: Arby’s manager charged with felony poisoning after customer claims tainted food caused herpes infection
events.description: An Oklahoma Arby's manager faces a felony poisoning charge after police say surveillance video allegedly showed her spitting into a customer's food.

events.title: California deputies use magnet-equipped drone to disarm knife-wielding sex offender hidden in garage
events.description: Austin Carter, a 30-year-old violent offender, had a SWAT team outside his cluttered home in Sacramento. Deputies used a drone to disarm him without risking the lives of officers.

events.title: Georgia Six Flags riders temporarily left dangling high in the air due to 'technical delay'
events.description: Passengers were stuck hanging out in the air on the SkyScreamer ride at Six Flags Over Georgia after what the park called a "technical delay"

events.title: Nancy Guthrie case could hinge on mystery tipster who emailed TMZ
events.description: A potential tipster in the Nancy Guthrie probe may be female despite using male pseudonyms, as experts say human sources may solve the unsolved case.

events.title: South Carolina fitness trainer made cryptic Instagram comments weeks before her body was found
events.description: South Carolina fitness trainer Elena Moore made mysterious Instagram comments about being free weeks before her body was found in Lexington County.

events.title: Colombia's 'El Tigre' secures presidency as leftist rival finally concedes defeat
events.description: After initially challenging the results, Colombia's leftist candidate conceded a razor-thin loss to conservative outsider Abelardo de la Espriella.

events.title: Turkey detains over 200 suspects, including alleged ISIS militants, in sweeping raid ahead of NATO summit
events.description: Turkey detained more than 200 suspects, including alleged ISIS militants, in a sweeping Ankara raid ahead of the July NATO summit Trump is expected to attend.

events.title: Trump gets major win against China in African rare earth minerals race
events.description: Trump administration backs American mining company Virtus Minerals in the DRC to counter China's control of critical cobalt and copper supply chains.

events.title: Trump says Colombia's 'El Tigre' will be a 'great president' as socialist opponent launches legal challenge
events.description: Trump congratulated de la Espriella on his apparent Colombia election win. With 99.9% counted, he leads 49.7% to 48.7%, but rival Cepeda has launched a challenge over voting irregularities.

events.title: Keith Kellogg tells Iranian dissidents the 'window is open' to force regime change in Tehran
events.description: Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg urged Iranian dissidents at a Paris NCRI gathering to seize a historic opening, saying the Tehran regime is weaker than ever.

events.title: Meloni's spat with Trump is calculated strategy to boost her approval ratings: expert
events.description: Analyst says Meloni's public feud with President Trump is a calculated strategy to boost her declining approval ratings ahead of Italy's 2027 general election.

events.title: Iran's unprecedented 'whole-regime' delegation at US deal talks signals one goal: expert
events.description: Iran sent a whole-regime delegation, including its central bank governor and oil officials to Switzerland talks, signaling demands for immediate cash flow.

events.title: Trump-backed 'El Tigre' looks to crush cartels, end Colombia's socialist era in pivotal election
events.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, known as El Tigre, rides voter frustration over crime and cartels in Colombia's high-stakes presidential runoff election.

events.title: African and Caribbean leaders call for payments, debt cancellation, formal apologies over slavery
events.description: African and Caribbean leaders adopted a 19-point reparations plan in Ghana demanding financial compensation, debt cancellation and formal apologies.
